domains.name: Foundation Model Integration and Solution Design

questions.stem:
An assistant must stream partial responses from a Bedrock model while preserving a multi-turn message history and tool-result messages for `support-chat-prod`.

The team is doing this after a 40% latency increase in production, without changing the approved data boundary, while preserving audit evidence for the release review, after a failed canary exposed missing rollback signals.

Which API should the developer use for the chat turn?

options.text: Create a Knowledge Base ingestion job for each user message
Call the Bedrock ConverseStream operation with the accumulated messages and inference configuration
Invoke an Agent action group directly without model orchestration
Start a batch inference job and poll S3 for partial tokens

questions.explanation:
**A** is incorrect because it solves a different lifecycle step and does not satisfy the stated runtime requirement. In this scenario, choosing `Create` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference.html).

**B** is correct because it matches the service behavior needed for the described architecture without adding unrelated components. The option aligns with `support-chat-prod` and the constraints stated in the scenario. See [Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference.html).

**C** is incorrect because it conflicts with the governance or latency constraint described in the scenario. In this scenario, choosing `Invoke` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference.html).

**D** is incorrect because it targets storage, ingestion, or monitoring rather than the decision point being tested. In this scenario, choosing `Start` would not meet the required behavior for `support-chat-prod`. See [Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference.html).
